title: The Popular Grimoire Tradition, 17th–19th c.
creator: various
blurb: The popular grimoire tradition — the *Grimorium Verum* (first printed 1817, tradition 17th-c.), *Grand Grimoire* or *Red Dragon*, the *Petit Albert* and *Grand Albert*, the *Sixth and Seventh Books of Moses*, and the *Black Pullet* — represents the commercialised street-level end of learned ceremonial magic. These books travelled across European chapbook culture, into African-American conjure (hoodoo), German-American *Braucherei* (pow-wow), and beyond, translated into the practical magic of specific communities. Their illustrations — spirit seals, letter squares, diagrammatic talismans, scenes of devil pacts — are among the most visually striking popular-press images of any century.
